events.reason: Story, Architecture, Spec, and implementation consistently separate delivery fact from reconciliation drift.
Reviewer directly inspected clean current HEAD and reran current-head typecheck and focused tests.
Immutable delivery, fail-closed behavior, exact identity, no cleanup, and additive rollback are encoded and tested.
Scope is limited to delivery/reconciliation, transaction/concurrency, and routed authority.
Parse, provider, retry, persistence, evidence lifecycle, and workflow regression paths map to code and tests.
Current-head typecheck, focused tests, guardrail tests, and E2E evidence satisfy done criteria.
Execute status/reconcile/merge exits, JSON fields, and recovery command are explicit and tested.
All-authority CAS, transaction-owned restoration, and newer operator state protection make rollback safe.
Common Story lock, authority routes, configured selectors, and sync boundaries are explicit.
Work is bounded projection and validation without added provider polling or persistence loops.
The focused atomic contract would lose shared identity, lock, CAS, and rollback guarantees if split.
Observe, recover, exit policy, local recovery, conflict handling, rollback, and next action are concrete.
Malformed provider JSON is persisted as blocked and is never inferred as success.
Provider failure does not invent delivery or erase prior observed delivery.
Sync failure exposes only execute reconcile and rejects invalid or stale recovery inputs.
Canonical and final persistence failures exit non-zero while preserving reconstructable delivery state.
Stale evidence is not treated as fresh; current-head verification and fail-closed projection are enforced.
Sync failure precedence, local authority preference, identity-bound fallback, and convergence are tested.

Dedicated auth_denied behavior is outside this Story's declared delivery/reconciliation boundary and lacks current-HEAD Story evidence; independent reviewer judged it non-release-blocking but not proven.

Authorization denial is outside this Story delivery/reconciliation boundary and dedicated evidence is not present; independent reviewer classified it non-blocking rather than inventing proof
Immutable delivery and mutable reconciliation remain consistently separated from Story through runtime.
The prior single-status model is concretely shown to misstate delivered facts and stale evidence.
Delivery identity, fail-closed reconciliation, and operator-state preservation are fixed invariants.
The Story excludes gate ledger redesign and stale-evidence regeneration, limiting itself to atomic recovery substrate.
Provider, persistence, synchronization, concurrency, routing, and evidence drift are independent failure surfaces.
Current-head typecheck, focused 48/48, and E2E 79/79 bind commands and raw artifacts.
JSON fields, recovery command, exits 0/1/2, and diagnostics are fixed in Spec and public-binary tests.
Rollback preserves additive delivery facts and restores only transaction-owned values.
Local, canonical, manifest, and linked-state write order and commit-last boundary are explicit.
Consistency is the dominant risk and the shared lock remains scoped per Story.
The large diff has one shared identity and transaction invariant with no second Story lineage.
Status, exact-selector reconcile, exits, degraded recovery, and rollback are operationally concrete.
Malformed provider or state JSON remains diagnostic evidence rather than success.
Non-zero provider results preserve blocked delivery evidence and retry context.
Retry is bound to stored base and PR identity and consumes only synchronization failure.

Provider-specific live authorization proof is outside this Story; generic provider failure is fail-closed, so final authorization operations remain human-owned and nonblocking for this implementation.
Partial persistence never becomes success and branches to rollback, local recovery, or explicit CAS conflict.
Current local authority suppresses stale canonical and other-HEAD evidence.
Delivered facts persist while unreconciled state stays non-zero until convergence.
Immutable delivery and mutable reconciliation are consistently connected from Story through implementation
Provider merged view base ancestry and current evidence are treated as separate authorities
Delivery non-reversal fail-closed evidence and cleanup protection are fixed by negative-path tests
State projection transaction concurrency and routed authority lanes match implementation boundaries
Exact-head E2E focused tests typecheck and architecture runtime reviews cover primary surfaces
Additive schema exit semantics JSON and legacy projection compatibility are fixed by shipped binary tests
CAS and per-write ownership protect newer operator state while preserving immutable delivery
Observation ancestry persistence linked synchronization reconciliation and lock order align in implementation and E2E
No added provider polling and bounded local validation preserves semantic correctness
CHANGELOG release guide status exit policy recovery command and rollback path align with public CLI E2E
Malformed provider data and corrupt execution state are isolated and fail closed
Provider failures do not infer delivery and preserve already observed delivery facts
Exact identity reconciliation converges without repeating delivery
Canonical failure local recovery rollback damage and CAS conflict remain distinct and nonzero
Same-head readiness priority stale Gate DAG rejection and strict-head evidence are tested
All fourteen exits use the shared CAS baseline finalizer and preserve both state axes
Current post-freeze E2E does not directly assert gate denial before provider operation for the auth boundary
The coherent change is still too large for one engineer to read consistently in a single review pass
Scenario text alone does not directly execute and assert provider operation denial in the post-freeze E2E
Ten ACs and nine scenarios map 23/23 to implementation and tests with no weak or unmapped lineage.
External delivery, current-head authorization, and operator-visible execution state remain distinct authorities.
Immutable delivery, fail-closed reconciliation, identity-bound recovery, CAS, and owned rollback are executable invariants.
Provider, gate authority, local/canonical/linked state, and execution state ownership are explicit.
Parse, provider, retry, auth denial, persistence, evidence lifecycle, and workflow-state failures have current executable evidence.
Strict-head E2E 11/11, focused 292/292, integration 2/2, and typecheck pass bind to ad4b4469.
Additive schema, compatible top-level status, CLI exits, and exact recovery command agree across spec and tests.
Non-destructive delivery rollback, transaction ownership, CAS conflict, and local degraded recovery are verified.
Lock, generation guard, heartbeat, CAS, follow-up transaction, and reconcile ordering are coherent.
The change is semantic rather than performance-oriented; synchronization meaning and contention safety are contract-tested.
Despite 56 files, 23/23 traceability forms one authority contract whose split would create incomplete states.
Release guide, status/reconcile operator path, nonzero exits, recovery, and additive rollback are present.
Malformed provider JSON and state fail closed into blocked or quarantine outcomes.
Provider failures do not infer delivery and persist a blocked result.
Retry idempotency, follow-up replay, exact identity, and duplicate avoidance are executable.
Missing, conflicting, stale, wrong-route, and missing-DAG authority stop before provider mutation.
Canonical and execution persistence failures preserve delivery and use owned rollback or local recovery.
Strict-head binding, stale authority rejection, current reviews, and 23/23 traceability prevent stale green reuse.
Delivery, reconciliation, and completion remain separate and unresolved state cannot over-promote readiness.
